headline: Four Iranian women’s team members set to return home after being granted asylum in Australia
snippet: Three more members of the Iranian women’s football squad have left Australia, having previously sought asylum after playing in the Women’s Asian Cup. Players Mona Hamoudi, Zahra Sarbali and a team sup
full_text: Three more members of the Iranian women’s football squad have left Australia, having previously sought asylum after playing in the Women’s Asian Cup. Players Mona Hamoudi, Zahra Sarbali and a team support staff member reportedly left Australia on Saturday night for Kuala Lumpur where they met up with other team members who are returning to Tehran. Iranian state media have also reported that the team’s captain, Zahra Ghanbari, has also withdrawn her bid for asylum, and will head from Australia to Malaysia and from there fly back to Iran. If Ghanbari joins her teammates in flying home, only two of the seven squad members who had sought asylum will remain. The IRNA news agency in Iran said the captain was “returning to the embrace of the homeland”, with the Mehr news agency seeing it as a “patriotic decision”. The Australian home affairs minister, Tony Burke, said on Sunday morning that three players had decided to join the rest of the team in Iran, after being issued special protection v...
